inputRoute: Built-in MacBook Air microphone; browser permission not granted
viewport: Desktop browser; controlled DevTools session (500 CSS px minimum)
assistiveTechnology: Chrome accessibility tree only; no screen reader
thermalObservation: No sustained active capture; no thermal or memory claim

layout.notes: Desktop content remained available and the setup controls were not clipped in the controlled session. The session did not establish 200% zoom, a 320 CSS px viewport, portrait, or landscape evidence.

permission-start.notes: Start was activated once by user action. Status changed from Ready to Requesting microphone access and then Microphone access was denied. The controlled Chrome profile did not permit granting access, so listening did not start.

pitch-history.notes: No live source was evaluated because microphone permission was denied. The accessibility tree exposed Waiting for a stable pitch and No recent stable notes only. No accuracy claim is made; issues #77 and #82 remain open.

transposition.notes: The instrument preference was changed by keyboard to B-flat trumpet while offline and the text alternative changed to Written pitch for B-flat trumpet. No live concert source was available, so written-note transposition was not validated.

calibration.notes: Calibration remained disabled because listening could not start.

filters.notes: A band-stop filter was added by keyboard while offline. Named enabled, type, frequency, width, reduction, remove, bypass, and reset controls were exposed. Enabled-versus-bypassed audio behavior could not be compared without microphone access.

monitor.notes: The opt-in monitor could not be exercised because listening did not start.

stop.notes: Listening never started, so track release and the operating-system microphone indicator could not be observed.

background.notes: No active capture session existed to background and resume.

screen-lock.notes: No active capture session existed to lock and recover.

interruption.notes: No controllable operating-system audio interruption was available without active capture.

permission-revocation.notes: The denied-permission error and retry guidance were understandable and focus returned to Start listening. Permission could not first be granted, so revocation from an allowed state was not tested.

route-change.notes: Only the built-in input was available to this run, and no active capture existed. No wired, Bluetooth, external-input removal, or route-switch claim is made.

keyboard.notes: Tab and Enter reached and expanded Setup with visible focus. Keyboard input changed the instrument preference and added a filter. Named controls, values, units, disabled states, and disclosure states were present in the accessibility tree. A complete pointer-free traversal and 200% zoom check were not completed, so this is not a pass.

screen-reader.notes: No VoiceOver, TalkBack, or other screen reader was used. Accessibility-tree inspection does not establish announcements. The tree exposed main, named regions, polite atomic status, staff/history alternatives, and setup controls; raw SVG content was not exposed in the snapshot.

privacy-network.notes: From initial load through denied Start and offline preference/filter changes, no fetch request was observed. Initial requests were first-party document, CSS, JavaScript, notation chunk, and a missing first-party favicon. Active-capture traffic was not inspected because microphone permission was denied, so the required privacy row remains blocked.

sustained.notes: No 30-minute active microphone run occurred. Battery endpoints, active-capture memory, responsiveness, dropped updates, and thermal behavior remain unmeasured.
